subUnits.title: 8.1 Reflection of Light, Laws, Image Formation by Mirrors & Applications
notes: Reflection of Light and Image Formation by Mirrors
Key Concepts:
1. Laws of Reflection:
- Incident ray, reflected ray, and normal lie in the same plane
- Angle of incidence (i) = Angle of reflection (r)
2. Plane Mirrors:
- Image characteristics: virtual, upright, same size, laterally inverted, same distance behind mirror
- Used in periscopes, dressing mirrors, kaleidoscopes
3. Curved Mirrors:
a) Concave (Converging):
- Real/inverted images (object beyond focus), virtual/upright (object within focus)
- Used in shaving mirrors, car headlights, solar concentrators
b) Convex (Diverging):
- Always virtual, upright, diminished images
- Wide field of view: used in car side mirrors, security mirrors
4. Mirror Formula & Magnification:
- 1/f = 1/u + 1/v (f = focal length, u = object distance, v = image distance)
- Magnification m = v/u = image height/object height
5. Nigerian Context:
- Convex mirrors on Nigerian roads for blind corners
- Periscope construction projects in SS2 Physics labs
- Shaving/makeup mirrors in Nigerian households
Conclusion:
- Reflection follows i = r and coplanar rays
- Plane mirrors produce virtual, upright, laterally inverted images
- Curved mirrors produce real/virtual images depending on object position
- Mastery supports optics, safety engineering, and daily applications

Common misconceptions and how to avoid them

quiz.q: The law of reflection states that:
quiz.explanation: Angle of incidence equals angle of reflection (i = r).

quiz.q: Images formed by plane mirrors are:
quiz.explanation: Plane mirrors produce virtual, upright, laterally inverted images of the same size.

quiz.q: A convex mirror always forms images that are:
quiz.explanation: Convex mirrors always produce virtual, upright, diminished images with a wide field of view.

quiz.q: In Nigerian context, convex mirrors are commonly used on roads for:
quiz.explanation: Convex mirrors provide wide-angle visibility, making them ideal for road safety at blind corners.
Magnification m = v/u = image height / object height.

quiz.q: A common misconception about concave mirrors is:
opts: They can form virtual images if object is within focus
They are used in car side mirrors
quiz.explanation: Concave mirrors form virtual, upright images when the object is placed within the focal length.
Lateral inversion means left and right sides are reversed in the mirror image.

quiz.q: To construct a periscope, you should use:
quiz.explanation: Periscopes use two plane mirrors angled at 45° to reflect light and allow viewing over obstacles.

quiz.q: When object distance equals focal length in a concave mirror, image is:
quiz.explanation: When object is at focus (u = f), reflected rays are parallel and image forms at infinity.
